title: Vegan chocolate cake
subtitle: A dark, moist sponge with coconut oil ganache
excerpt: A vegan chocolate cake that does not compromise: deep cocoa flavour, a moist tender crumb from oil and oat milk, and a dark chocolate ganache made with coconut cream. No eggs, no dairy.
sourceNotes: Eggless chocolate cakes appear in American wartime baking (the wacky cake or crazy cake, dating to the 1940s, uses vinegar and bicarbonate of soda for lift in place of eggs). Modern vegan versions developed this method with plant milks replacing water and oils replacing butter. This recipe draws on the wacky cake tradition documented in Isa Chandra Moskowitz's Veganomicon (2007) and Meera Sodha's East (2019), with a coconut cream ganache replacing the traditional dairy version.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the ganache, wrapped in cling film, for up to 2 months. Ganache and serve after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cake keeps well in an airtight tin for 3 days. Ganache on the day of serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: dark chocolate coconut cream ganache poured over the cooled cake

content.text: The vinegar in the batter reacts with the bicarbonate of soda to create lift in the same way that buttermilk and egg whites do in a dairy cake. It leaves no vinegar flavour in the baked result. Do not skip it: the cake will be flatter and denser without it.

Refrigerate a tin of full-fat coconut milk overnight before making the ganache. The solid cream separates to the top and can be scooped out; it has a much higher fat content than the liquid and will set the ganache correctly.

attrs.prepNote: from the top of a refrigerated can of full-fat coconut milk

content.text: Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 20 cm round cake tin. Sift together the {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} into a large bowl. In a jug, combine the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{oat-milk}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{apple-cider-vinegar}}. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and whisk until smooth. Pour into the tin and bake for 32 to 38 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 15 minutes, then turn out onto a rack to cool completely.

Place the {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl. Heat the {{coconut-cream}} in a small saucepan until it just begins to simmer, then pour over the chocolate. Leave for 2 minutes, then stir from the centre outward until smooth. Leave to cool at room temperature for 20 to 30 minutes until the ganache thickens to a pourable coating. Set the cooled cake on a rack over a tray and pour the ganache over the top, letting it run down the sides. Leave undisturbed for 30 minutes before cutting.
